briefs.title: BC Hydro electricity rates will rise to cover North Coast Transmission Line construction costs
briefs.summary: BC's government has directed the BC Utilities Commission (the independent body that oversees electricity rates) to approve new rate schedules that let BC Hydro recover the full costs of building the North Coast Transmission Line — a major new power line running from Prince George to Bob Quinn Lake. This means BC Hydro can pass those construction costs on to customers through higher electricity rates, and the Commission cannot block or reduce that cost recovery.
briefs.action_required: Review your electricity bills and budget for potential BC Hydro rate increases tied to the North Coast Transmission Line project.
briefs.industries: Natural Resources, Construction & Development, Transportation & Logistics
